title_original: Quantum simulates properties of the first-ever half-Möbius molecule, designed by IBM and researchers
summary_original: Researchers from IBM, Oxford, the University of Manchester, ETH Zurich, École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne, and the University of Regensburg have designed and built the first molecule with a half-Möbius electronic topology—a previously unconceived form of quantum matter—assembled atom by atom at IBM Research Europe – Zurich. This quantum system, C₁₃Cl₂, can be switched between topological states and was characterized using advanced IBM-developed techniques including scanning tunneling and atomic force microscopy. Traditional classical methods proved inadequate for simulating its complex electronic properties. Instead, the team used the SqDRIFT algorithm on IBM's quantum-centric supercomputer, performing calculations on up to 100 qubits to decode its quantum structure—demonstrating quantum computers as essential instruments for interpreting experimental results in quantum chemistry.
title_fixed: Quantum simulates properties of the first-ever half-Möbius molecule, designed by IBM and researchers

Traditional classical methods proved inadequate for simulating its complex electronic properties. Instead, the team used the SqDRIFT algorithm on IBM's quantum-centric supercomputer, performing calculations on up to 100 qubits to decode its quantum structure—demonstrating quantum computers as essential instruments for interpreting experimental results in quantum chemistry.
